attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- canvas-dev-tools: This skill provides Canvas LMS development utilities for testing LTI applications. It should be used when users need to create mock students with SIS IDs, accept course invitations in bulk, manage test enrollments, or perform other Canvas development tasks. All operations execute inline Ruby code via docker compose.
- dart-financial-analysis: This skill provides tools for analyzing South Korean corporate financial data from DART (Data Analysis, Retrieval and Transfer System). This skill should be used when users ask about Korean company financials, disclosures, business information, or want to compare financial metrics of Korean listed companies (KOSPI/KOSDAQ).
- template-skill: Replace with description of the skill and when Claude should use it.
- fedify-activitypub: Guide for building and maintaining ActivityPub/fediverse applications using Fedify. This skill should be used when users want to create federated social applications, implement ActivityPub protocols, build new features, test and debug federation, deploy to production, look up FEPs, or maintain existing Fedify applications.
- skill-share: A skill that creates new Claude skills and automatically shares them on Slack using Rube for seamless team collaboration and skill discovery.
- agent-creator: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create an agent", "write a subagent", "generate agent definition", "add agent to plugin", "write agent frontmatter", "create autonomous agent", "build subagent", needs agent structure guidance, YAML frontmatter configuration, invocation criteria with examples, or wants to add specialized subagents to Claude Code plugins with proper capabilities lists and tool access definitions.
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
- lsp-config: This skill should be used when the user wants to view, add, remove, or modify language server configurations in `.lsp.json`. It should be used when the user mentions `.lsp.json`, LSP config, language server setup, or wants to configure type-checking/linting tools for their project.
- obsidian:defuddle: Extract clean markdown content from web pages using Defuddle CLI, removing clutter and navigation to save tokens. Use instead of WebFetch when the user provides a URL to read or analyze, for online documentation, articles, blog posts, or any standard web page.
- obsidian:obsidian-cli: Interact with Obsidian vaults using the Obsidian CLI to read, create, search, and manage notes, tasks, properties, and more. Also supports plugin and theme development with commands to reload plugins, run JavaScript, capture errors, take screenshots, and inspect the DOM. Use when the user asks to interact with their Obsidian vault, manage notes, search vault content, perform vault operations from the command line, or develop and debug Obsidian plugins and themes.
- obsidian:obsidian-bases: Create and edit Obsidian Bases (.base files) with views, filters, formulas, and summaries. Use when working with .base files, creating database-like views of notes, or when the user mentions Bases, table views, card views, filters, or formulas in Obsidian.
- obsidian:json-canvas: Create and edit JSON Canvas files (.canvas) with nodes, edges, groups, and connections. Use when working with .canvas files, creating visual canvases, mind maps, flowcharts, or when the user mentions Canvas files in Obsidian.
- obsidian:obsidian-markdown: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.
- minimalism-workflow:commit: Create a git commit with auto-generated message from staged changes. Use when the user wants to commit, create a commit, or says "commit".
- minimalism-workflow:load-plan: Load a previously saved plan or spec for the current project directory. This skill should be used when the user wants to load a plan, recall a spec, review a saved design document, or says "load-plan".
- minimalism-workflow:gh-release-note: Generate a release changelog from git commits between the previous tag and HEAD, then create a GitHub release. This skill should be used when the user wants to write release notes, generate a changelog, create a GitHub release, or summarize what changed since the last release.
- minimalism-workflow:cleanup-plans: Clean up saved plans from the planvault. This skill should be used when the user wants to delete old plans, remove stale specs, prune the planvault, or says "cleanup-plans".
- minimalism-workflow:release-tag: Create a git tag from the project's version file (package.json, Cargo.toml, pubspec.yaml, build.gradle, etc.). This skill should be used when the user wants to tag a release, create a version tag, or tag the current commit for release.
- minimalism-workflow:gh-issue-filer: This skill should be used when the user wants to file a GitHub issue, create a bug report, request a feature, report a problem, or open an issue in the current repository. It explores the codebase to understand project context, gathers issue details through conversation, and files the issue via the gh CLI. Trigger phrases include "file an issue", "create an issue", "report a bug", "request a feature", "open an issue", or "gh issue".
- minimalism-workflow:save-plan: Save a plan or spec as markdown, associated with the current project directory. This skill should be used when the user wants to save a plan, persist a spec, store a design document, or says "save-plan".
- minimalism-workflow:list-plans: List all saved plans from the planvault, optionally filtered by the current project. This skill should be used when the user wants to see available plans, browse saved specs, or says "list-plans".
- minimalism-workflow:ghpr: Create a GitHub pull request with auto-generated title and body from commits. Use when the user wants to create a PR, open a pull request, or says "ghpr".
- minimalism-workflow:store-release-note: Generate concise App Store / Play Store release notes from the latest GitHub release. This skill should be used when the user wants to write app store release notes, play store changelog, or compress release notes for mobile store submission.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt:
Explore this Android app codebase to find the article detail page/screen and understand how edit functionality is implemented for other entities.

Specifically:
1. Find the article detail screen/composable. Articles are likely a type of post. Look in `app/src/main/java` for things like `ArticleDetail`, `ArticleDetailScreen`, or similar. Also find any `PostDetailScreen` to see the pattern.
2. Find any existing "edit" button implementations on related screens (e.g., post detail, profile detail) — especially how they detect that the current user is the author, how the button is rendered, and how it navigates to the edit screen.
3. Check if there's already an article edit screen (the branch name is `fix/article-edit-functionality` and recent commits added EditProfileScreen — so there may be an existing article edit screen already). If found, note its route/navigation.
4. Report: (a) file path + line numbers for the ArticleDetailScreen (or equivalent), (b) how ownership is determined (viewer vs actor), (c) whether/where an Edit Article screen already exists, and (d) any existing edit button pattern to follow from profile or post detail.

Thorough level: medium. Report under 300 words with concrete file paths and line numbers.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the key information. Let me compile the findings:

## Exploration Results

Based on my analysis of the Android app codebase, here are the findings:

### 1. **Article Detail Screen**
Articles are shown via the **PostDetailScreen** (not a separate ArticleDetailScreen). The screen detects articles by checking `post.typename == "Article"` (line 562, PostDetailScreen.kt).

**File**: `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android-worktrees/fix-article-edit-functionality/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/screens/postdetail/PostDetailScreen.kt`

**Line numbers**:
- Lines 299-300: Title displays "Article" vs "Post"
- Lines 562-575: Article-specific rendering (larger title, divider)
- Lines 677-691: "Read on Web" button for articles

### 2. **Ownership Determination (canDelete Pattern)**
Ownership is determined by comparing the viewer's handle with the post actor's handle:

**File**: `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android-worktrees/fix-article-edit-functionality/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/screens/postdetail/PostDetailViewModel.kt`

**Lines 82-85** (and 114-116 on refresh):
```kotlin
val viewerHandle = sessionManager.userHandle.first()
val canDelete = viewerHandle != null &&
    result.post.actor.handle.equals(viewerHandle, ignoreCase = true) &&
    result.post.sharedPost == null
```

The deletion is hidden in a **MoreVert menu** (lines 309-323, PostDetailScreen.kt), shown only if `canDelete` is true.

### 3. **EditArticleScreen Status**
**No ArticleEditScreen exists yet**. Only `EditProfileScreen` exists. The ComposeArticleScreen is used for creating new articles and loading drafts via `draftId` parameter, but there's no dedicated edit-article screen for editing published articles.

**File**: `/Users/kodingwarrior/fediverse/hackerspub-android-worktrees/fix-article-edit-functionality/app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/ui/screens/compose/ComposeArticleScreen.kt` (lines 64-69 show draft loading but no published article edit).

### 4. **Edit Button Pattern**
The pattern is in **ProfileScreen** (lines 642-654):
- Check `isViewer` boolean (set in ViewModel based on session handle vs profile handle)
- Show **OutlinedButton** labeled "Edit Profile" if true
- Trigger `onEditProfileClick()` callback
- Navigation in HackersPubApp.kt routes to `DetailScreen.EditProfile.route` (lines 588-606)

**Navigation Route**: `DetailScreen.EditProfile : DetailScreen("edit-profile")` (line 146, HackersPubApp.kt)

For articles, a similar pattern would create `DetailScreen.EditArticle : DetailScreen("edit-article/{articleId}")` and pass `onEditArticleClick` callback from PostDetailScreen.

466	) {

477

482	
483	    val navBarBottom = WindowInsets.navigationBars.asPaddingValues().calculateBottomPadding()
484	
485	    LazyColumn(

487	    ) {
488	        item {
489	            Column(
490	                modifier = Modifier.padding(12.dp)
491	            ) {
492	                // Reply target preview

518	                            style = typography.labelSmall,

522	
523	                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(4.dp))
524	                    HorizontalDivider(color = colors.divider)
525	                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(12.dp))
526	                }
527	
528	                Row(
529	                    verticalAlignment = Alignment.CenterVertically
530	                ) {
531	                    AsyncImage(

536	                            .clip(CircleShape)
537	                            .clickable { onProfileClick(post.actor.handle) },
538	                        contentScale = ContentScale.Crop

540	
541	                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.width(12.dp))
542

122	fun PostDetailScreen(
123	    postId: String,
124	    onNavigateBack: () -> Unit,
125	    onProfileClick: (String) -> Unit,
126	    onReplyClick: (String) -> Unit,
127	    onQuoteClick: (String) -> Unit = {},
128	    onPostClick: (String) -> Unit,
129	    isLoggedIn: Boolean = true,
130	    viewModel: PostDetailViewModel = hiltViewModel()
131	) {
132	    val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState()
133	    val context = LocalContext.current
134	    val colors = LocalAppColors.current
135	    val confirmBeforeDelete by viewModel.preferencesManager.confirmBeforeDelete.collectAsState(
136	        initial = true
137	    )
138	    val confirmBeforeShare by viewModel.preferencesManager.confirmBeforeShare.collectAsState(initial = false)
139	    var showDeleteConfirmation by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
140	    var showShareConfirmation by remember { mutableStateOf(false) }
141	    var webViewUrl by remember { mutableStateOf<String?>(null) }
142	
143	    // Navigate back after successful deletion
144	    LaunchedEffect(uiState.isDeleted) {
145	        if (uiState.isDeleted) {
146	            onNavigateBack()
147	        }
148	    }
149	
150	    // WebView bottom sheet
151	    if (webViewUrl != null) {
152	        ModalBottomSheet(
153	            onDismissRequest = { webViewUrl = null },
154	            sheetState = rememberModalBottomSheetState(skipPartiallyExpanded = true)
155	        ) {
156	            WebViewSheetContent(url = webViewUrl!!)
157	        }
158	    }
159	
160	    // Reaction picker bottom sheet
161	    if (uiState.showReactionPicker) {
162	        ModalBottomSheet(
163	            onDismissRequest = { viewModel.toggleReactionPicker() },
164	            sheetState = rememberModalBottomSheetState(skipPartiallyExpanded = true)
165	        ) {
166	            ReactionPicker(
167	                reactionGroups = uiState.reactionGroups,
168	                isSubmitting = uiState.isReacting,
169	                onEmojiSelect = { viewModel.toggleReaction(it) },
170	                onClose = { viewModel.toggleReactionPicker() }
171	            )
172	        }
173	    }
174	
175	    // Shares bottom sheet
176	    if (uiState.showSharesSheet) {
177	        ModalBottomSheet(
178	            onDismissRequest = { viewModel.dismissSharesSheet() },
179	            sheetState = rememberModalBottomSheetState(skipPartiallyExpanded = true)
180	        ) {
181	            SharesSheet(
182	                actors = uiState.shareActors,
183	                isLoading = uiState.isLoadingShares,
184	                onProfileClick = { handle ->
185	                    viewModel.dismissSharesSheet()
186	                    onProfileClick(handle)
187	                },
188	                onClose = { viewModel.dismissSharesSheet() }
189	            )
190	        }
191	    }
192	
193	    // Reactors bottom sheet
194	    if (uiState.showReactorsSheet && uiState.reactionGroups.isNotEmpty()) {
195	        val initialIndex = uiState.selectedReactionGroup
196	            ?.let { selected -> uiState.reactionGroups.indexOf(selected).coerceAtLeast(0) }
197	            ?: 0
198	        ModalBottomSheet(
199	            onDismissRequest = { viewModel.dismissReactorsSheet() },
200	            sheetState = rememberModalBottomSheetState(skipPartiallyExpanded = true)
201	        ) {
202	            ReactorsSheet(
203	                groups = uiState.reactionGroups,
204	                initialIndex = initialIndex,
205	                onProfileClick = { handle ->
206	                    viewModel.dismissReactorsSheet()
207	                    onProfileClick(handle)
208	                }
209	            )
210	        }
211	    }
212	
213	    // Quotes bottom sheet
214	    if (uiState.showQuotesSheet) {
215	        ModalBottomSheet(
216	            onDismissRequest = { viewModel.dismissQuotesSheet() },
217	            sheetState = rememberModalBottomSheetState(skipPartiallyExpanded = true)
218	        ) {
219	            QuotesSheet(
220	                posts = uiState.quotePosts,
221	                isLoading = uiState.isLoadingQuotes,
222	                onPostClick = { id ->
223	                    viewModel.dismissQuotesSheet()
224	                    onPostClick(id)
225	                },
226	                onClose = { viewModel.dismissQuotesSheet() }
227	            )
228	        }
229	    }
230	
231	    if (showDeleteConfirmation) {
232	        AlertDialog(
233	            onDismissRequest = { showDeleteConfirmation = false },
234	            title = { Text(stringResource(R.string.delete_post_confirm_title)) },
235	            text = { Text(stringResource(R.string.delete_post_confirm)) },
236	            confirmButton = {
237	                TextButton(
238	                    onClick = {
239	                        showDeleteConfirmation = false
240	                        viewModel.deletePost()
241	                    }
242	                ) {
243	                    Text(
244	                        stringResource(R.string.delete_post),
245	                        color = colors.reaction
246	                    )
247	                }
248	            },
249	            dismissButton = {
250	                TextButton(onClick = { showDeleteConfirmation = false }) {
251	                    Text(stringResource(R.string.cancel))
252	                }
253	            }
254	        )
255	    }
256	
257	    if (showShareConfirmation) {
258	        val hasShared = uiState.post?.viewerHasShared == true
259	        AlertDialog(
260	            onDismissRequest = { showShareConfirmation = false },
261	            title = {
262	                Text(stringResource(if (hasShared) R.string.unshare_confirm_title else R.string.share_confirm_title))
263	            },
264	            confirmButton = {
265	                TextButton(
266	                    onClick = {
267	                        showShareConfirmation = false
268	                        if (hasShared) viewModel.unsharePost() else viewModel.sharePost()
269	                    }
270	                ) {
271	                    Text(stringResource(if (hasShared) R.string.unshare_confirm_action else R.string.share_confirm_action))
272	                }
273	            },
274	            dismissButton = {
275	                TextButton(onClick = { showShareConfirmation = false }) {
276	                    Text(stringResource(R.string.cancel))
277	                }
278	            }
279	        )
280	    }

@Composable
fun ComposeArticleScreen(
    draftId: String? = null,
    onSaveSuccess: () -> Unit,
    onPublishSuccess: (articleId: String) -> Unit,
    onNavigateBack: () -> Unit,
    viewModel: ComposeArticleViewModel = hiltViewModel()
) {
    val uiState by viewModel.uiState.collectAsState()
    val snackbarHostState = remember { SnackbarHostState() }

    val colors = LocalAppColors.current
    val typography = LocalAppTypography.current

    val titleFocusRequester = remember { FocusRequester() }
    val contentFocusRequester = remember { FocusRequester() }
    val keyboardController = LocalSoftwareKeyboardController.current

    LaunchedEffect(draftId) {
        draftId?.let { viewModel.loadDraft(it) }
    }

    LaunchedEffect(uiState.isSaved) {
        if (uiState.isSaved) {
            snackbarHostState.showSnackbar("Draft saved")
            viewModel.clearSavedFlag()
        }
    }

    LaunchedEffect(uiState.isPublished) {
        if (uiState.isPublished) {
            val articleId = uiState.publishedArticleId
            if (articleId != null) {
                onPublishSuccess(articleId)
            } else {
                onSaveSuccess()
            }
        }
    }

    LaunchedEffect(uiState.error) {
        uiState.error?.let {
            snackbarHostState.showSnackbar(it)
            viewModel.clearError()
        }
    }

    LaunchedEffect(Unit) {
        if (draftId == null) {
            titleFocusRequester.requestFocus()
        }
    }

    val saveEnabled = uiState.title.isNotBlank() && !uiState.isSaving && !uiState.isPublishing
    val publishEnabled = uiState.title.isNotBlank() && !uiState.isSaving && !uiState.isPublishing

    Scaffold(
        contentWindowInsets = WindowInsets(0),

lastPrompt: Add edit button for article detail page

content.text:
I've explored the code. Here's what I found:

- Article detail uses `PostDetailScreen` (it's a `Post` with `typename == "Article"`). `canDelete` already detects ownership via `viewerHandle` vs `actor.handle` in `PostDetailViewModel.kt:82-85`.
- The existing topBar action is a `MoreVert` dropdown menu (`PostDetailScreen.kt:309-323`, `405-446`) with only a Delete item.
- There is **no** edit-article screen or repository mutation yet. `ComposeArticleScreen` loads `draftId` but not a published article.

Before I code: what should the edit button actually do? Two reasonable paths:

1. **Add button + full edit flow** — extend the compose flow to load a published article and wire an `updateArticle` mutation. A single larger change.
2. **Add button + navigate to Compose with articleId** — still needs the compose-side wiring to do anything useful, so essentially the same work.

Given the memory note that fine-grained commits must each stay functional, I don't want to land a button that no-ops. Want me to do the full flow (button + load published article in ComposeArticle + publish-as-edit mutation), or should I scope this PR to just UI wiring and leave the mutation for a follow-up?

content.text:
I verified against the live GraphQL endpoint at `hackers.pub/graphql` — the server exposes no article-edit mutation. Only `saveArticleDraft`, `publishArticleDraft`, `deleteArticleDraft` exist. There is no way for a client to update a published article today.

Given my "fine-grained must stay working" rule, I can't land just a UI button that no-ops. Three realistic options:

1. **Clone-as-draft flow** — Edit button converts the article to a new draft (pre-filled from article content) and opens ComposeArticle. Publishing creates a *new* article; the old one stays. Semantically wrong but buildable client-side only.
2. **Block on backend** — file an issue on the server repo for an `updateArticle` mutation, then resume here. Stop this branch.
3. **UI-only scaffolding** — land the button disabled with a "coming soon" snackbar. Violates the rule but leaves placement in place.

Which direction? I'd lean toward (2) since (1) misleads users about what "edit" does.

content.subject: Add Edit menu + navigation in PostDetailScreen
content.description: Add canEdit to PostDetailUiState (viewer owns article AND typename == Article). Add Edit item to PostDetailActionMenu dropdown. Thread onEditClick callback from screen. Wire route in HackersPubApp navigation to pass articleId to ComposeArticle.

content.subject: Update GraphQL schema with updateArticle + rawContent
content.description: Add updateArticle mutation, UpdateArticleInput, UpdateArticlePayload, UpdateArticleResult to Mutation type, plus rawContent to ArticleContent in app/src/main/graphql/pub/hackers/android/schema.graphqls. Source: ../../../hackerspub/graphql/schema.graphql

content.subject: Add UpdateArticle mutation + ArticleForEdit query
content.description: In operations.graphql add a mutation UpdateArticle and a query to fetch the editable article data (title, tags, language, allowLlmTranslation, and contents.rawContent). Run apollo codegen (./gradlew :app:generateApolloSources or similar) to generate Kotlin classes.

content.subject: Add updateArticle + getEditableArticle repo methods
content.description: Add suspend fun updateArticle(...) and suspend fun getEditableArticle(articleId): Result<EditableArticle> to HackersPubRepository. Model EditableArticle with title, content, tags, language, allowLlmTranslation.

content.subject: Extend ComposeArticleViewModel with edit mode
content.description: Add articleId field to UI state. When articleId is set, load via getEditableArticle on init. Add updateArticle() method that calls repo.updateArticle; on success emit isPublished=true with publishedArticleId=articleId. Guard against trying to save-as-draft in edit mode.

content.subject: Extend ComposeArticleScreen for edit mode
content.description: Accept articleId param. In edit mode: show "Update" instead of Save/Publish, skip slug field (articles already have slug), show language + allowLlmTranslation fields. Call viewModel.updateArticle() on submit.

class HackersPubRepository @Inject constructor(

package pub.hackers.android.domain.model

import androidx.compose.runtime.Immutable
import java.time.Instant

@Immutable
data class Actor(
    val id: String,
    val name: String?,
    val handle: String,
    val avatarUrl: String,
    val bio: String? = null
)

@Immutable
data class ActorField(
    val name: String,
    val value: String
)

@Immutable
data class AccountLink(
    val name: String,
    val handle: String?,
    val icon: String,
    val url: String,
    val verified: String?
)

@Immutable
data class Media(
    val url: String,
    val thumbnailUrl: String?,
    val alt: String?,
    val height: Int?,
    val width: Int?,
    val mediaType: String? = null
) {
    val isVideo: Boolean get() = mediaType?.startsWith("video/") == true
}

@Immutable
data class EngagementStats(
    val replies: Int,
    val reactions: Int,
    val shares: Int,
    val quotes: Int
)

@Immutable
data class PostLinkImage(
    val url: String,
    val alt: String?,
    val width: Int?,
    val height: Int?
)

@Immutable
data class PostLink(
    val title: String?,
    val description: String?,
    val url: String,
    val siteName: String?,
    val author: String?,
    val image: PostLinkImage?,
    val creator: Actor?
)

@Immutable
data class Post(
    val id: String,
    val typename: String,
    val name: String?,
    val published: Instant,
    val summary: String?,
    val content: String,
    val excerpt: String,
    val url: String?,
    val iri: String? = null,
    val viewerHasShared: Boolean,
    val actor: Actor,
    val media: List<Media>,
    val link: PostLink? = null,
    val engagementStats: EngagementStats,
    val mentions: List<String>,
    val lastSharer: Actor? = null,
    val sharersCount: Int = 0,
    val sharedPost: Post? = null,
    val replyTarget: Post? = null,
    val quotedPost: Post? = null,
    val visibility: PostVisibility = PostVisibility.PUBLIC,
    val reactionGroups: List<ReactionGroup> = emptyList()
)

enum class PostVisibility {
    PUBLIC, UNLISTED, FOLLOWERS, DIRECT, NONE
}

@Immutable
data class NotificationPost(
    val id: String,
    val content: String
)

sealed class Notification {
    abstract val id: String
    abstract val uuid: String
    abstract val created: Instant
    abstract val actors: List<Actor>

    @Immutable
    data class Follow(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>
    ) : Notification()

    @Immutable
    data class Mention(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: NotificationPost?
    ) : Notification()

    @Immutable
    data class Reply(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: NotificationPost?
    ) : Notification()

    @Immutable
    data class Quote(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: NotificationPost?
    ) : Notification()

    @Immutable
    data class Share(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val post: NotificationPost?
    ) : Notification()

    @Immutable
    data class React(
        override val id: String,
        override val uuid: String,
        override val created: Instant,
        override val actors: List<Actor>,
        val emoji: String?,
        val customEmoji: CustomEmoji?,
        val post: NotificationPost?
    ) : Notification()
}

@Immutable
data class CustomEmoji(
    val id: String,
    val name: String,
    val imageUrl: String
)

@Immutable
data class ReactionGroup(
    val emoji: String?,
    val customEmoji: CustomEmoji?,
    val count: Int,
    val reactors: List<Actor>,
    val viewerHasReacted: Boolean = false
)

@Immutable
data class Viewer(
    val id: String,
    val username: String,
    val name: String,
    val bio: String,
    val avatarUrl: String,
    val handle: String
)

@Immutable
data class LoginChallenge(
    val token: String
)

@Immutable
data class Session(
    val id: String,
    val account: Account
)

@Immutable

1352	    suspend fun getArticleDraft(id: String): Result<ArticleDraft> {
1353	        return try {
1354	            val response = apolloClient.query(ArticleDraftQuery(id))
1355	                .fetchPolicy(FetchPolicy.NetworkOnly)
1356	                .execute()
1357	
1358	            if (response.hasErrors()) {
1359	                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
1360	            } else {
1361	                val draft = response.data?.articleDraft
1362	                    ?: return Result.failure(Exception("Draft not found"))
1363	                Result.success(
1364	                    ArticleDraft(
1365	                        id = draft.id,
1366	                        title = draft.title,
1367	                        content = draft.content.toString(),
1368	                        tags = draft.tags,
1369	                        created = Instant.parse(draft.created.toString()),
1370	                        updated = Instant.parse(draft.updated.toString())
1371	                    )
1372	                )
1373	            }
1374	        } catch (e: Exception) {

content.subject: Add UpdateArticle mutation + ArticleForEdit query
content.description: In operations.graphql add a mutation UpdateArticle and a query to fetch the editable article data (title, tags, language, allowLlmTranslation, and contents.rawContent). Run apollo codegen (./gradlew :app:generateApolloSources or similar) to generate Kotlin classes.

content.subject: Add updateArticle + getEditableArticle repo methods
content.description: Add suspend fun updateArticle(...) and suspend fun getEditableArticle(articleId): Result<EditableArticle> to HackersPubRepository. Model EditableArticle with title, content, tags, language, allowLlmTranslation.

content.subject: Extend ComposeArticleViewModel with edit mode
content.description: Add articleId field to UI state. When articleId is set, load via getEditableArticle on init. Add updateArticle() method that calls repo.updateArticle; on success emit isPublished=true with publishedArticleId=articleId. Guard against trying to save-as-draft in edit mode.

content.subject: Extend ComposeArticleScreen for edit mode
content.description: Accept articleId param. In edit mode: show "Update" instead of Save/Publish, skip slug field (articles already have slug), show language + allowLlmTranslation fields. Call viewModel.updateArticle() on submit.

content.subject: Add Edit menu + navigation in PostDetailScreen
content.description: Add canEdit to PostDetailUiState (viewer owns article AND typename == Article). Add Edit item to PostDetailActionMenu dropdown. Thread onEditClick callback from screen. Wire route in HackersPubApp navigation to pass articleId to ComposeArticle.

content.subject: Update GraphQL schema with updateArticle + rawContent
content.description: Add updateArticle mutation, UpdateArticleInput, UpdateArticlePayload, UpdateArticleResult to Mutation type, plus rawContent to ArticleContent in app/src/main/graphql/pub/hackers/android/schema.graphqls. Source: ../../../hackerspub/graphql/schema.graphql

content.subject: Add UpdateArticle mutation + ArticleForEdit query
content.description: In operations.graphql add a mutation UpdateArticle and a query to fetch the editable article data (title, tags, language, allowLlmTranslation, and contents.rawContent). Run apollo codegen (./gradlew :app:generateApolloSources or similar) to generate Kotlin classes.

content.subject: Add updateArticle + getEditableArticle repo methods
content.description: Add suspend fun updateArticle(...) and suspend fun getEditableArticle(articleId): Result<EditableArticle> to HackersPubRepository. Model EditableArticle with title, content, tags, language, allowLlmTranslation.

content.subject: Extend ComposeArticleViewModel with edit mode
content.description: Add articleId field to UI state. When articleId is set, load via getEditableArticle on init. Add updateArticle() method that calls repo.updateArticle; on success emit isPublished=true with publishedArticleId=articleId. Guard against trying to save-as-draft in edit mode.

content.subject: Extend ComposeArticleScreen for edit mode
content.description: Accept articleId param. In edit mode: show "Update" instead of Save/Publish, skip slug field (articles already have slug), show language + allowLlmTranslation fields. Call viewModel.updateArticle() on submit.

All six commits land clean on `fix/article-edit-functionality`. Build and tests pass at each step.

Flow: owner + Article → MoreVert menu now shows Edit → navigates to `ComposeArticle?articleId=…` → loads title/content/tags/language via `ArticleForEdit` query → Update button calls `updateArticle` mutation → pops back.

Caveat: the live `hackers.pub` server hasn't deployed the `updateArticle` mutation yet (only merged to `main` server-side). So the final mutation will 400 until the server is redeployed — the UI and flow are wired and ready.

69	class HackersPubRepository @Inject constructor(
70	    private val apolloClient: ApolloClient
71	) {
72	    suspend fun getPublicTimeline(after: String? = null, refresh: Boolean = false): Result<TimelineResult> {
73	        return try {
74	            val response = apolloClient.query(PublicTimelineQuery(Optional.presentIfNotNull(after)))
75	                .apply { if (refresh) fetchPolicy(FetchPolicy.NetworkOnly) }
76	                .execute()
77	
78	            if (response.hasErrors()) {
79	                Result.failure(Exception(response.errors?.firstOrNull()?.message ?: "Unknown error"))
80	            } else {
81	                val data = response.data?.publicTimeline
82	                withContext(Dispatchers.Default) {
83	                    Result.success(
84	                        TimelineResult(
85	                            posts = data?.edges?.map { edge ->
86	                                edge.node.postFields.toPost(edge.node.sharedPost?.sharedPostFields?.toPost())
87	                            }?.distinctBy { it.id } ?: emptyList(),
88	                            hasNextPage = data?.pageInfo?.hasNextPage ?: false,
89	                            endCursor = data?.pageInfo?.endCursor
90	                        )
91	                    )
92	                }
93	            }
94	        } catch (e: Exception) {
95	            Result.failure(e)
96	        }
97	    }
98

65	
66	    // Locally-composed replies appended optimistically after a successful reply
67	    // from this screen. Rendered after the paginated replies; cleared on refresh
68	    // so the server-authoritative list wins.

70	    val locallyAddedReplies: StateFlow<List<Post>> = _locallyAddedReplies.asStateFlow()
71	
72	    // Replies are paginated independently of the main post payload. The main
73	    // post, reactionGroups, and sheet/delete/translation state stay in UiState
74	    // because they are single-instance optimistic updates that don't benefit
75	    // from the PagingData overlay pattern.
76	    val replies: Flow<PagingData<Post>> =
77	        cursorPager { after -> repository.postRepliesPage(postId, after) }
78	            .flow
79	            .distinctByEffectiveId()
80	            .cachedIn(viewModelScope)
81	
82	    init {
83	        loadPost(postId)
84	        viewModelScope.launch {
85	            replyPostedSignal.events.collect { event ->
86	                if (event.replyTargetId == postId) {
87	                    appendLocalReply(event.reply)
88	                }
89	            }
90	        }
91	    }
92	
93	    private fun appendLocalReply(reply: Post) {
94	        _locallyAddedReplies.update { it + reply }
95	        _uiState.update { state ->
96	            val post = state.post ?: return@update state
97	            state.copy(
98	                post = post.copy(
99	                    engagementStats = post.engagementStats.copy(
100	                        replies = post.engagementStats.replies + 1
101	                    )
102	                )
103	            )
104	        }
105	    }

    $articleId: ID!,
    $title: String,
    $content: Markdown,
    $tags: [String!],
    $language: Locale,
    $allowLlmTranslation: Boolean
) {
    updateArticle(input: {
        articleId: $articleId,
        title: $title,
        content: $content,
        tags: $tags,
        language: $language,
        allowLlmTranslation: $allowLlmTranslation
    }) {
        ... on UpdateArticlePayload {
            article {
                id
                name
                url
            }
        }
        ... on InvalidInputError {
            inputPath
        }
        ... on NotAuthenticatedError {
            notAuthenticated
        }
    }
}

message.content: How can I edit my article???

attachment.stdout: OK

content.text:
Right now you can't — the Edit menu item is gated behind `ARTICLE_EDIT_ENABLED` which is `false` in `app/src/main/java/pub/hackers/android/FeatureFlags.kt:6`. Two options:

1. **Flip the flag locally** to test the UI — set `ARTICLE_EDIT_ENABLED = true`, rebuild. The Edit item appears in the MoreVert menu on any Article you own. But the `updateArticle` mutation isn't on prod `hackers.pub` yet, so submitting will fail with "Cannot query field updateArticle". Only useful against a local/staging server running `hackers-pub/hackerspub@main`.

2. **Wait for server deploy** — once prod has `updateArticle` + `rawContent`, flip the flag in a follow-up commit.

Want me to flip it now for local testing?

content.text:
Flag flipped to `true`. Open an article you authored → MoreVert menu → Edit.

Unstaged in worktree, not committed — flip it back before merging upstream. Submitting will fail against live prod until `updateArticle` is deployed.

Now, about the scroll-to-top button placement — I wired the action menu next to it during conflict resolution. Let me re-read that section.

attachment.stdout: Output truncated (0KB total). Full output saved to: REDACTED.output

content.text:
Trailing row now shows TOC → ScrollToTop → MoreVert for an Article you own — 3 icons is crowded, and ScrollToTop sandwiched between a content-navigation icon and an ownership action reads awkwardly.

A few options:

1. **Move ScrollToTop to a FAB-style floating button** that fades in only when scrolled past the fold. Keeps the top bar to 2 icons (TOC + MoreVert) and makes scroll-to-top more discoverable.
2. **Hide ScrollToTop in the top bar when the action menu is visible.** Cheap, but the button was useful; hiding it for owners is inconsistent.
3. **Reorder: MoreVert first, then TOC, then ScrollToTop.** Groups ownership actions to the right-most cluster matches Material convention of overflow-menu-last — but ScrollToTop at the far right feels equally wrong.

I lean toward (1). Want me to go that route, or pick another?

lastPrompt: Wait, We can't setup the original content. See ../hackerspub's approach

content.text:
Confirmed the bug. Web-next selects the **original** content (`originalLanguage == null`) from `contents[]`, and reads `title` + `rawContent` + `language` from that `ArticleContent`. My repo picks either the first one or one matching `article.language` — which can be a translation, so the editor shows translated/stale text.

Fix: update the query to include `contents.title` + `contents.originalLanguage`, and select `contents.find { it.originalLanguage == null }`.

content.text:
Confirmed — live `hackers.pub/graphql` still doesn't expose `rawContent` on `ArticleContent`:

```
['beingTranslated', 'content', 'id', 'language', 'originalLanguage',
 'published', 'summary', 'summaryStarted', 'title', 'toc', ...]
```

The server-side PR (commit `32b0d75` — *"Add updateArticle mutation and rawContent field"*) is merged to `hackerspub` main but the production host hasn't been redeployed. The Apollo request is failing validation on the server, not in our client. No client-side fix will change this.

Options:
1. **Point the app at a local server** running `hackerspub@main` — edit `AppModule.kt:65` `.serverUrl("https://hackers.pub/graphql")` to your local host and rebuild.
2. **Ask whoever owns deploys to redeploy prod** — the server code is ready.
3. **Leave the flag off** until (2) lands. The gating I wired is exactly for this window.

Do you have a local `hackerspub` server running I should point the app at?

file.content:
version: "3.8"

services:
  app:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.dev
    environment:
      DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:password@db:5432/hackerspub
      KV_URL: file:///tmp/kv.db
      SECRET_KEY: ${SECRET_KEY}
      BEHIND_PROXY: ${BEHIND_PROXY}
      MAILGUN_KEY: ${MAILGUN_KEY}
      MAILGUN_REGION: ${MAILGUN_REGION}
      MAILGUN_DOMAIN: ${MAILGUN_DOMAIN}
      MAILGUN_FROM: ${MAILGUN_FROM}
      SENTRY_DSN: ${SENTRY_DSN}
      PLAUSIBLE: ${PLAUSIBLE}
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    depends_on:
      - db
    command: deno task dev
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - /app/deps
      - /app/.cache
      - /app/.deno

  web-next:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.dev
    environment:
      NO_WATCHMAN: "1"
      VITE_API_URL: http://app:8000/graphql
      HOST: "0.0.0.0"
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    depends_on:
      - app
    command: sh -c "deno task -f web-next codegen && deno task -f web-next dev"
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - /app/deps
      - /app/.cache
      - /app/.deno

  db:
    image: postgres:17
    environment:
      POSTGRES_USER: postgres
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: password
      POSTGRES_DB: hackerspub
    volumes:
      - db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data

volumes:
  db_data:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<manifest xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android"
    xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools">

    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET" />
    <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS" />

    <application
        android:name=".HackersPubApplication"
        android:allowBackup="true"
        android:enableOnBackInvokedCallback="true"
        android:icon="@mipmap/ic_launcher"
        android:label="@string/app_name"
        android:roundIcon="@mipmap/ic_launcher_round"
        android:supportsRtl="true"
        android:theme="@style/Theme.HackersPub"
        tools:targetApi="33">
        <activity
            android:name=".MainActivity"
            android:exported="true"
            android:theme="@style/Theme.HackersPub">
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.MAIN" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.LAUNCHER" />
            </intent-filter>
            <intent-filter>
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
                <data
                    android:scheme="hackerspub"
                    android:host="verify" />
            </intent-filter>
            <intent-filter android:autoVerify="true">
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
                <data
                    android:scheme="https"
                    android:host="hackers.pub"
                    android:pathPattern="/@.*" />
            </intent-filter>
            <intent-filter android:autoVerify="true">
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
                <data
                    android:scheme="https"
                    android:host="hackers.pub"
                    android:pathPrefix="/sign/in/" />
            </intent-filter>
            <intent-filter android:autoVerify="true">
                <action android:name="android.intent.action.VIEW" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.DEFAULT" />
                <category android:name="android.intent.category.BROWSABLE" />
                <data
                    android:scheme="https"
                    android:host="hackers.pub"
                    android:pathPrefix="/tags/" />
            </intent-filter>
        </activity>
        <provider
            android:name="androidx.startup.InitializationProvider"
            android:authorities="${applicationId}.androidx-startup"
            tools:node="remove" />
    </application>

</manifest>

package pub.hackers.android.ui.screens.compose

import android.content.Context
import android.os.Build
import android.view.textclassifier.TextClassificationManager
import android.view.textclassifier.TextLanguage
import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
import dagger.hilt.android.lifecycle.HiltViewModel
import dagger.hilt.android.qualifiers.ApplicationContext
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.update
import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
import pub.hackers.android.data.repository.HackersPubRepository
import javax.inject.Inject

data class ComposeArticleUiState(
    val title: String = "",
    val content: String = "",
    val tags: String = "",
    val draftId: String? = null,
    val articleId: String? = null,
    val isEditMode: Boolean = false,
    val isLoadingArticle: Boolean = false,
    val isSaving: Boolean = false,
    val isSaved: Boolean = false,
    val slug: String = "",
    val language: String = java.util.Locale.getDefault().language,
    val allowLlmTranslation: Boolean = true,
    val showPublishFields: Boolean = false,
    val isPublishing: Boolean = false,
    val isPublished: Boolean = false,
    val publishedArticleId: String? = null,
    val publishedArticleUrl: String? = null,
    val error: String? = null
)

@HiltViewModel
class ComposeArticleViewModel @Inject constructor(
    private val repository: HackersPubRepository,
    @ApplicationContext private val context: Context
) : ViewModel() {

content.text:
Two fine-grained commits on the branch:

- `1ee20f1` — Load original ArticleContent when editing article (picks `originalLanguage == null` like web-next)
- `ba69206` — Add markdown preview toggle to ComposeArticleScreen

In the compose/edit screen there's now an **Edit / Preview** pill row above the content surface. Preview renders `markdownToHtml(content)` via `HtmlContent`. Empty content shows `compose_preview_empty`. Applies to new drafts, loaded drafts, and the article edit flow.
